title: crepis arcuata
intro: Crepis arcuata, also known as Sharp-leaved Hawksbeard or Sharp-leaved Hawksbeard, is a plant species in the dandelion family (Asteraceae) native to Europe and Western Asia. The plant is used in traditional medicine due to its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory and antiseptic compounds that can help soothe digestive issues and wounds. The health benefits of Crepis arcuata include its ability to reduce fever, alleviate digestive problems, and support the immune system. The herbal preparations of Crepis arcuata typically involve infusions or decoctions of the plant's leaves and roots, which can be used to make teas or applied topically as a poultice.
